task_desc: Build interactive 3D Drosophila brain model with real-time connectome activity -- Add a toggleable full-screen panel containing a Three.js 3D brain visualization that shows the fly's neural activity in real time. The brain should be a stylized but anatomically-informed representation of the Drosophila brain with distinct volumes for each major neuropil region, colored by functional type (sensory blue, central purple, drives amber, motor red). Each region's opacity/emissive glow should be driven by the live connectome activation levels so users can watch the brain "light up" as the fly behaves. Implementation: (1) Add Three.js via CDN (no build step) in index.html, (2) Create js/brain3d.js with a Brain3D module that initializes a WebGL scene, camera (perspective with orbit controls via Three.js OrbitControls), ambient + point lighting, and a renderer sized to its container, (3) Model the brain regions as positioned/scaled meshes using Three.js built-in geometries (SphereGeometry, ellipsoids via scaled spheres, TorusGeometry for mushroom body lobes) arranged in anatomically approximate positions: optic lobes (VIS_*) as two large lateral hemispheres, antennal lobes (OLF_*) as two smaller anterior spheres, mushroom bodies (MB_*) as distinctive paired peduncle+calyx+lobe structures in the dorsal-posterior brain, central complex (CX_*) as a midline disc/fan structure, lateral horn (LH_*) as paired lateral volumes, subesophageal zone (SEZ_*, GNG_*) as a ventral mass, ventral nerve cord (VNC_*, DN_*, MN_*) as an elongated ventral cylinder, (4) Use MeshPhongMaterial or MeshStandardMaterial with transparency (opacity: 0.3 base) and emissive color; each mesh gets an update() method that reads the relevant BRAIN.postSynaptic neuron states, averages them, and maps to emissive intensity (0.0-1.0) and opacity (0.3-0.8), (5) Add a toggle button to the toolbar ("Brain 3D") that shows/hides the 3D panel as an overlay, (6) Add hover detection via raycasting: when the user hovers a region, show a tooltip with the region name, description, and list of neuron groups it contains with their current activation levels, (7) Render loop calls Brain3D.update() each frame, reading from BRAIN.postSynaptic to update all region glows. Region-to-neuron mapping: Optic Lobes = [VIS_R1R6, VIS_R7R8, VIS_ME, VIS_LO, VIS_LC, VIS_LPTC], Antennal Lobes = [OLF_ORN_FOOD, OLF_ORN_DANGER, OLF_LN, OLF_PN], Mushroom Bodies = [MB_KC, MB_APL, MB_MBON_APP, MB_MBON_AV, MB_DAN_REW, MB_DAN_PUN], Central Complex = [CX_EPG, CX_PFN, CX_FC, CX_HDELTA], Lateral Horn = [LH_APP, LH_AV], SEZ = [SEZ_FEED, SEZ_GROOM, SEZ_WATER, GUS_GRN_SWEET, GUS_GRN_BITTER, GUS_GRN_WATER], VNC/Motor = [DN_WALK, DN_FLIGHT, DN_TURN, DN_BACKUP, DN_STARTLE, VNC_CPG, MN_*], Thermosensory = [THERMO_WARM, THERMO_COOL], Mechanosensory = [MECH_BRISTLE, MECH_JO, MECH_CHORD, ANTENNAL_MECH], Drives = [DRIVE_HUNGER, DRIVE_FEAR, DRIVE_FATIGUE, DRIVE_CURIOSITY, DRIVE_GROOM]. Files: index.html, js/brain3d.js (new), css/main.css.
